TASK.TITLE: Fix Studio Access Guard Mismatch for EDITOR Role
TASK.DESCRIPTION: Resolve the authorization mismatch issue where users with EDITOR role are blocked from accessing the /studio flow in the frontend, while the expected policy for Cluster 2 allows AUTHOR or EDITOR roles for edit operations. This task requires aligning frontend guard logic with the intended access policy and validating consistency with backend rules.
TASK.PROBLEM_STATEMENT:
Current /studio access is restricted to AUTHOR only at multiple layers in the frontend: - Server page guard (server-auth.ts) - Page entry guard (page.tsx) - Client component guard (draft-studio.tsx)
Meanwhile, Cluster 2 policy expects AUTHOR and EDITOR to both have access to edit flows. Backend article management also currently enforces AUTHOR-only restrictions, creating a mismatch between expected policy and actual implementation.

FIX_REQUIREMENTS.ITEM: Update frontend guards to support AUTHOR and EDITOR roles instead of AUTHOR-only
Ensure consistent access control across: - proxy layer - server page guard - client component guard
Align guard logic so that EDITOR is no longer incorrectly blocked at frontend level
Verify that role-based access logic is centralized and not duplicated inconsistently

BACKEND_ALIGNMENT.ITEM: Review backend authorization rules for article management
If necessary, align backend to support AUTHOR and EDITOR roles for edit operations
Ensure no unintended privilege escalation occurs

VALIDATION.ITEM: AUTHOR can access /studio and perform edit operations
EDITOR can access /studio and perform edit operations (if aligned with policy)
Non-authorized roles are still blocked appropriately

CODE_CHANGES.ITEM: Updated frontend guard logic to support AUTHOR and EDITOR roles
Refactored duplicated guard checks if necessary

FILE.NAME: ikp-bug2.xml
FILE.DESCRIPTION: Structured XML describing the bug, root cause, and fix approach

FILE.NAME: bug2-report.txt
FILE.DESCRIPTION: Human-readable report summarizing the issue, analysis, and resolution

CONSTRAINTS.ITEM: Follow existing project patterns for guards and auth utilities
Do not introduce breaking changes to unrelated routes
Keep changes minimal and focused on fixing the role mismatch
Reports must reference observed behavior and actual code analysis
Place report files inside bug-issue folder
Use similar structure and tone as existing bug reports in the repository
